name: crypt
tagline: Authenticated encryption, password hashing, and signing for Go — safe defaults, stdlib-only, with byte-for-byte TypeScript interop.
description: crypt is a batteries-included cryptography toolkit for Go: AES-256-GCM and ChaCha20-Poly1305 authenticated encryption, argon2id/bcrypt password hashing, HMAC and Ed25519 signing, HKDF key derivation, key rotation (KeyRing), envelope encryption (KMS), streaming AEAD, and CSPRNG helpers — wrapped around the standard library with safe defaults and a versioned wire format that is byte-for-byte interoperable with the TypeScript counterpart crypt-ts.

positioning.audience: Go backend engineers who need correct crypto without hand-rolling nonces, modes, and KDFs.
positioning.wedge: Encrypt a database column (or sign a webhook) in one line, with authentication built in and the same bytes readable from a Node service.
differentiators: Authenticated by default (AES-256-GCM / ChaCha20-Poly1305) — no silent CBC-without-HMAC footguns.
Versioned wire format so new algorithms slot in without breaking decrypt of old data.
Byte-for-byte interop with the TypeScript twin crypt-ts via a shared known-answer vector file.
Batteries included: KeyRing rotation, KMS envelope encryption, streaming AEAD, argon2id/bcrypt, HKDF, Ed25519.
Zero third-party crypto — thin, auditable wrappers over the Go standard library and golang.org/x/crypto.
